plant.description: Unique History: A plant with a mystical and dual history in India. Associated with Lord Shiva, it symbolizes 'destruction of ego' and 'transformed poison'. Classified under the 'Upavisha' (semi-toxic) group in ancient Ayurvedic treatises like Susruta Samhita. Used in 'Shodhana' (purification) processes to neutralize its toxicity. Physical Characteristics: A perennial herb growing up to 1-2 meters in height, with a spreading crown and dark-grey, rough stems. It features broad, heart-shaped (cordate) leaves with wavy margins and large, trumpet-shaped white or purple flowers. The fruit is a globose, spiny capsule containing flattened yellowish-brown seeds. It is found as a wild weed in wastelands and near habitations throughout India. Specific Chemical Compounds: Primarily contains Tropane Alkaloids (Scopolamine, Hyoscyamine) and Withanolides. The presence of scopolamine (hyoscine) is crucial for its neuro-relaxant, anti-motion-sickness, and anti-spasmodic effects. Strict Standardizations: The seed and leaves are standardized in API based on macroscopic features (trumpet flowers, spiny fruit). Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<10%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>5%), and water-soluble extractive (>15%). HPTLC quantification is used to monitor total alkaloid content calculated as hyoscyamine. Purification Process (Shodhana): The seeds are traditionally soaked in cow's milk (Dugdha-shodhana) for several hours followed by washing with hot water to remove excessive toxic alkaloids before medicinal use.

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Prominently pacifies Vata and Kapha doshas. Due to its Tikta (bitter) and Katu (pungent) Rasa and Ushna (heating) Virya, it is the most prestigious herb for 'Shwasahara' (asthma-relieving) and 'Vedanasthapana' (pain-relieving) therapies using its 'Madaka' (narcotic) potency. It may significantly aggravate Pitta and cause systemic drying (Ruksha).

purified_seed_powder__churna_.method: 60 mg to 125 mg of fine powder (extremely low dose) taken with honey once daily for asthma.

kanakasava.method: 5-10ml of fermented extract taken twice daily after meals for managing chronic cough and asthma symptoms.

leaf_paste__lepa_.method: Ground fresh leaves apply directly to localized swellings, boils, and joint pain to reduce heat and pain.

medicated_oil__taila_.method: Seeds are processed with coconut/sesame oil; used for local scalp massage (dandruff) and joint pain.

medicated_smoke__dhumapana_.method: Dried leaf pieces are burnt, and the smoke is inhaled (with extreme caution) to relieve acute asthma spasms.

dosage.children: Strictly NOT recommended for children under any circumstances due to toxicity.
dosage.adults: Powder (Purified): 60-125 mg; Leaf juice: 10-20 drops (external use); Kanakasava: 5-10 ml, divided throughout the day before or after meals.
dosage.elderly: Very low doses (30-60mg) are advisable initially, monitoring for any increase in dizziness or dry mouth.

medicinal_uses: Expert Antiasthmatic and Bronchodilator: Dhattura is the premier Ayurvedic remedy for acute bronchial asthma and spasmodic cough. Its 'Vyavayi' (swift) and 'Vikasi' (tissue-loosening) nature provided by tropane alkaloids induces rapid relaxation of bronchiole muscles. WHO-aligned research recognizes its potent anticholinergic and bronchodilatory effects.

Pain and Neuralgia Management: Acts as a powerful local anesthetic and analgesic 'Vedanasthapana'. Medicated oils and leaf pastes are traditionally applied to provide instant relief from sciatica, rheumatoid arthritis pain, and neuralgic headaches.

Dandruff and Scalp Care: The 'Kandughna' (anti-itch) and antimicrobial properties of the seeds are used to treat chronic dandruff, lice, and scalp infections. It is traditionally boiled in coconut oil for topical scalp massage.

Fever and Malaria Relief: Traditionally used in very small, purified doses in 'Kanakasava' to manage intermittent fevers. It helps in reducing the intensity of shivers and body aches through its systemic Vata-pacifying action.

Internal Parasites: Its 'Krimighna' (worm-destroying) property is utilized primarily in external applications to resolve localized parasitic skin infections and internally (with extreme caution) for specific intestinal parasites.

Anti-inflammatory and Antiseptic: Crushed leaves applied topically as a 'Lepa' (paste) act as a potent anti-inflammatory for boils, mumps, and localized swellings to reduce pain and expedite suppuration.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Tropane Alkaloids (Hyoscyamine, Scopolamine, Atropine), Withanolides, and Niacin. The presence of hyoscyamine and scopolamine is crucial for its narcotic, analgesic, and bronchodilatory action.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: NOT classified as GRAS. It is a potent semi-poison (Upavisha) and must only be used by qualified Ayurvedic physicians. National and International regulations apply to its collection and sale.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: Strictly contraindicated in individuals with glaucoma, acute constipation, high Pitta, heart diseases, and during pregnancy. Toxicity is high; non-purified use is lethal.
possible_side_effects: Overdose results in dry mouth, dilated pupils, blurry vision, hallucinations, delirium, racing heart, and respiratory failure. Discontinue if dry mouth occurs.
drug_interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anticholinergics, sedatives, or heart medications as it causes severe potentiation and toxicity. No safe levels documented without professional guidance.
